Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: ceremonial weapons [300263273], armaments [300420324], weapons [300036926], Weapons and Ammunition (hierarchy name) [300036743], Furnishings and Equipment (hierarchy name) [300264551], Objects Facet [300264092]

Non-Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: swords by function [300037100], swords [300037048], edged weapons [300036973], armaments [300420324], weapons [300036926], Weapons and Ammunition (hierarchy name) [300036743], Furnishings and Equipment (hierarchy name) [300264551], Objects Facet [300264092]

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: Swords that play a part in public state or civic ceremonies or rituals, being variously worn, carried, or presented, as symbols of honor or power.
Espadas que juegan un rol en ceremonias oficiales o cívicas, o rituales, siendo a veces usadas, otras veces portadas, o presentadas, como símbolos de honor o poder.

Zwaarden die een rol spelen in openbare staatsaangelegenheden of officiële ceremonies of rituelen en die afwisselend op het lichaam of in de hand worden gedragen, of worden overhandigd als symbolen van eer of macht.
在公開的政府或民間典禮或儀式中所使用的劍，其佩帶、攜帶或呈現的方式各有不同，作為榮譽或權力的象徵。

Source.Source_ID: 2000031521/Oakeshott, Sword in the Age of Chivalry (1965)

Term_Source.Page: illustration, 52, ill. p. 53 and elsewhere in book

Source.Source_ID: 2000039541/Tarassuk and Blair, Complete Encyclopedia of Arms and Armor (1979)
